name: Needle lace
description: built with needle and thread over a drawn pattern, often using buttonhole stitches, bars, and decorative fillings

name: Bobbin lace
description: made by twisting and crossing many threads wound on bobbins, producing braided paths and mesh grounds

name: Chantilly lace
description: fine bobbin lace known for delicate floral motifs, outlined details, and light net grounds

name: Alencon lace
description: needle lace with corded outlines, floral motifs, and luxurious raised detail

name: Guipure lace
description: heavy lace with motifs connected by bars or brides instead of net ground

name: Venise lace
description: dense raised lace, often floral or scroll-like, with ornate heavy motifs and open connectors

name: Brussels lace
description: fine historical lace with delicate floral motifs and separate motifs often applied to net

name: Torchon lace
description: bobbin lace with geometric grounds, fans, spiders, diamonds, and practical repeated structure

name: Cluny lace
description: bobbin lace with bold geometric motifs, wheels, tallies, leaves, and braided texture

name: Honiton lace
description: English bobbin lace with separately made floral sprigs joined into compositions

name: Valenciennes lace
description: fine bobbin lace with integrated motif and mesh, soft continuous detail

name: Binche lace
description: fine bobbin lace with snowflake-like grounds and delicate continuous patterns

name: Duchesse lace
description: bobbin lace combining floral motifs, open spaces, and decorative fillings

name: Reticella
description: early needle lace evolved from cutwork, using rigid geometric grids, squares, circles, and radiating forms

name: Punto in aria
description: needle lace detached from fabric ground, giving freer openwork structure

name: Cutwork lace
description: fabric openwork made by cutting holes and stitching around the edges

name: Drawn-thread lace
description: openwork made by removing fabric threads and bundling or stitching remaining threads

name: Filet lace
description: square net grid with filled and open cells used to make pictorial patterns

name: Filet crochet
description: crochet grid of open and filled squares, excellent for charts and pixel-like lace motifs

name: Crochet lace
description: hook-made lace using chains, shells, fans, mesh, picots, clusters, and motifs

name: Irish crochet lace
description: raised crochet motifs such as roses, leaves, and shamrocks joined by mesh

name: Pineapple lace
description: crochet lace pattern using expanding shell and chain forms that resemble pineapples or teardrops

name: Hairpin lace
description: long looped strips made on a fork and joined into airy lace fabric

name: Broomstick lace
description: large loops lifted onto a rod and grouped into open fanlike clusters

name: Knitted lace
description: lace made through yarn overs and decreases, often used for shawls and open motifs

name: Shetland lace
description: very fine knitted lace with traditional motifs, airy shawls, and delicate repeats

name: Tatting
description: knotted rings and chains with picots, made by shuttle or needle

name: Macrame-adjacent lace
description: knot-based openwork with heavier cord, useful as a neighboring style

name: Raschel lace
description: machine-made warp-knit lace with flexible modern repeat structures

name: Leavers lace
description: machine lace that imitates complex handmade lace with fine detail

name: Schiffli lace
description: machine embroidery lace made on net or dissolved ground

name: Chemical lace
description: embroidered lace made on a sacrificial ground that dissolves away

name: Embroidered net lace
description: net fabric decorated with stitched motifs and outlines

name: Broderie anglaise
description: whitework cutwork with eyelets, embroidery, and scalloped edges

name: Crochet doilies
description: radial crochet lace objects with rounds, fans, shells, picots, and medallions

name: Lace edgings
description: border lace designed for hems, cuffs, collars, trims, and insertions

name: Lace insertions
description: narrow lace strips inserted between fabric panels

name: Allover lace
description: continuous lace field used as fabric rather than isolated trim

name: Corded lace
description: lace with heavier outline thread emphasizing motifs

name: Stretch lace
description: elastic modern lace used in lingerie and fitted apparel

name: Bridal lace
description: luxury lace language for veils, gowns, appliques, trains, and heirloom motifs
